competition.narrative: Three competitors/alternatives are confirmed across calls, Next_Step, and Contact Roles. (1) SAP Ariba is the incumbent procurement platform being evaluated for replacement. The unnamed CPO requested 'Ariba replacement use cases' and 'win back on ex Ariba customers' collateral on 12 December 2025 (Next_Step 'Dec 12nd CPO requests for some Ariba replacement use cases. Demo scheduled by mid Jan. Currently sharing collaterals on Delta and win back on ex Ariba customers'). The Next_Step entry '28 January Good engagement with the relevant stakeholder interests on Spend management and AI (on top of Ariba/to be reviewed)' confirms Ariba is actively being reviewed for replacement, positioning it as the INCUMBENT THREAT. Ariba is the platform Zycus must displace to win the S2P opportunity. (2) Unnamed competing risk management tool: Michelle Chung stated 'we are in parallel actively considering other risk management tool as well' on the 22 December 2025 call, confirming a live peer competitor for the ESG/risk module. This tool is unnamed, but it is ACTIVE and being evaluated in parallel with the relevant stakeholder Radar for the same 5-10 supplier pilot validation. Threat level is HIGH because it may have completed technical validation while Zycus NDA/pilot stalled for six months. (3) Do-nothing / status quo: Michelle stated their current 'shield' due diligence tool is in pilot phase ('still under pilot phase for certain procurement categories'), and despite its gaps, inertia is a real alternative. She said 'we don't have any concrete timeline' and 'we hope we can start deploying next year', signaling no forcing function and low urgency. The wedge against Ariba is AI/Merlin capabilities (Next_Step '28 January: Main interests on Spend management and AI (on top of Ariba)') plus Ariba replacement use cases (Delta story, win-backs). The wedge against the unnamed risk tool is Zycus's integrated S2P + ESG/risk platform (single system vs point solution), but this wedge is UNACTIVATED because the dual-track misalignment (Michelle vs Madalena) has not been resolved. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the unnamed competing risk management tool, which may be advancing while Zycus is stalled on NDA/pilot.

identify_pain.narrative: Michelle Chung (Supply Chain ESG Manager) owns the ESG compliance pain and articulated it clearly on the 22 December 2025 call. She stated: 'EU regulations require disclosure of supply chain ESG practices and modern slavery compliance', 'modern slavery is the red line', and the current due diligence tool ('the shield') 'lacks visibility into ESG incidents, especially modern slavery' and 'misses non-legal ESG risks and media-reported incidents'. She explained that 'most ESG incidents are not being processed in the courts or having legal proceedings. Some are allegations or annual reports uncovering these stories. It could pose a lot of reputational risk for us, but the current tool might not be enough to safeguard us from this risk.' The pain is operational (manual, incomplete risk visibility) and compliance-driven (EU CSRD/CSDDD disclosure requirements, modern slavery acts). Michelle also stated they need to 'disclose in the next reporting cycle on more effort being made in the supply chain' and 'start deploying the risk mapping solution next year', creating a soft forcing function tied to ESG reporting cycles. However, the pain owner at the EB/CFO level is unidentified, and no quantified cost of the pain (audit penalties, reputational damage, manual effort) was stated. The broader S2P platform pain (Ariba replacement) is referenced in Next_Step ('CPO requests for some Ariba replacement use cases', 12 December 2025; 'Main interests on Spend management and AI (on top of Ariba/to be reviewed)', 28 January 2026) but the Ariba pain owner, quantified impact, and pain intensity are unknown. The ESG pain is confirmed and Michelle-owned; the S2P platform pain is inferred and Madalena/CPO-owned but not detailed.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is split across two parallel tracks with no evidence of convergence. Track 1 (ESG/risk): Michelle Chung (Supply Chain ESG Manager) is evaluating standalone risk-profiling tools, including Zycus AI Risk Radar and at least one unnamed competing risk management tool ('we are in parallel actively considering other risk management tool as well', 22 December 2025 call). Michelle reports to Mark Watts (GM Procurement) but has autonomy on the ESG workstream. Track 2 (S2P platform): Madalena Lei (Head of Procurement Excellence, COE) is leading a broader procurement platform review to potentially replace SAP Ariba. The Next_Step log states 'Jan 28th Good engagement with the relevant stakeholder interests on Spend management and AI (on top of Ariba/to be reviewed). A 12months opportunity with ability to influence and good executive engagement. Next steps on deep dive demos to be confirmed by end of Feb.' This suggests a 12-month decision cycle anchored to the relevant stakeholder evaluation, but no unified timeline or decision authority is documented. Guillaume (Zycus) explicitly pushed for alignment on the 22 December call: 'we are looking for the big pictures around procurement in general' and asked Ruchi to contact Madalena to plan the platform review. However, no follow-up occurred, and the two tracks remain unlinked as of 1 July 2026. The risk is that Michelle's ESG module cannot justify the $300k spend alone, and if Madalena's S2P evaluation selects another vendor or stalls, Zycus loses both tracks. The process is partial because the dual-track structure is clear but the convergence mechanism, approval chain, and unified decision authority (EB/CPO/CFO) are unknown.

She said the current 'shield' tool 'lacks visibility into ESG incidents' and 'misses non-legal ESG risks', so the decision criteria center on proactive adverse-media monitoring and AI-powered risk alerting. Michelle also asked for methodology transparency ('white paper on AI Risk Radar scoring methodology and data sources') and quality validation via a 5-10 real supplier pilot. However, the broader S2P platform evaluation criteria (Madalena's COE workstream) are unknown: no RFP, no BRD, no shortlist criteria surfaced. The Next_Step log references 'pain points on Sourcing, Intake, Spend' (20 March 2026 entry) but no structured evaluation framework was shared. The dual-track structure (Michelle's ESG/risk vs Madalena's S2P) creates a criteria gap: if Zycus is evaluated only on the ESG/risk module, the decision may favor a standalone risk tool; if evaluated on the full S2P suite, the criteria and scoring are undefined. The move is to align the two tracks, surface the unified BRD/RFP from the relevant stakeholder, and map how the ESG/risk module fits the broader platform selection.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Differentiated where it counts — Buyer is AI CURIOUS with pockets of AI HUNGRY interest, but the signal is module-specific and not yet unified. Michelle Chung demonstrated AI HUNGRY interest in the AI Risk Radar (ESG/adverse media/modern slavery screening powered by AI) on the 22 December 2025 call, asking deep methodology questions ('what kind of data do you consider', 'what are the data sources', 'is the AI risk radar owned by Zycus or external') and requesting a 5-10 supplier pilot to validate AI output quality. The Next_Step logs show AI CURIOUS interest at the COE level: '28 January: Main interests on Spend management and AI (on top of Ariba/to be reviewed)', and the I2O (Merlin Intake) demos were scheduled ('I2O demo by Jan 26', '5 January: I2O Demo scheduled by Jan 21'), signaling interest in AI-powered intake automation. However, the broader Merlin agentic capabilities (autonomous sourcing agents, negotiation agents, spend analytics) were not discussed on any deep call, and it is unclear whether the COE or EB/CPO level understands the full AI value proposition. The risk is that AI appetite is module-specific (Risk Radar yes, I2O maybe, agentic sourcing/negotiation unknown) and has not been elevated to a unified 'AI-native S2P platform' narrative that displaces Ariba and justifies the $300k spend. Without AIS scoring (AIS_Score__c / AIS_Status__c are null in Salesforce), the AI fit signal is inferred from call content and Next_Step logs only.

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Three competitors/alternatives confirmed, recency-weighted and ranked by CURRENT threat. (1) Unnamed competing risk management tool is the SINGLE STRONGEST CURRENT THREAT (high, active): Michelle Chung stated 'we are in parallel actively considering other risk management tool as well' on 22 December 2025, positioning it as a live peer for the ESG/risk module pilot. This tool may have advanced while Zycus NDA/pilot stalled for six months, creating risk that the unnamed tool completes validation first. (2) SAP Ariba is the INCUMBENT (medium, being replaced): the unnamed CPO requested Ariba replacement use cases on 12 December 2025, and Next_Step logs confirm Ariba is under active review for replacement ('on top of Ariba/to be reviewed', 28 January 2026). Ariba is the platform Zycus must displace to win the S2P opportunity, but displacing an incumbent is a MEDIUM threat because the buyer has already signaled replacement intent. (3) Do-nothing / status quo is LOW/DORMANT: Michelle stated 'we don't have any concrete timeline' and the current 'shield' tool is 'still under pilot phase', signaling inertia is real but not preferred. The wedge against Ariba is AI/Merlin capabilities and integrated S2P + ESG/risk (single system vs Ariba + separate risk tool); the wedge against the unnamed risk tool is the same integrated platform value, but this wedge is UNACTIVATED because the dual-track misalignment has not been resolved. Zycus must re-engage Madalena, unify the two tracks, and execute NDA + pilot to neutralize the unnamed risk tool threat.

competitive_position.summary: Three competitors/alternatives confirmed, recency-weighted and ranked by CURRENT threat. (1) Unnamed competing risk management tool is the SINGLE STRONGEST CURRENT THREAT (high, active): Michelle Chung stated 'we are in parallel actively considering other risk management tool as well' on 22 December 2025, positioning it as a live peer for the ESG/risk module pilot. This tool may have advanced while Zycus NDA/pilot stalled for six months, creating risk that the unnamed tool completes validation first. (2) SAP Ariba is the INCUMBENT (medium, being replaced): the unnamed CPO requested Ariba replacement use cases on 12 December 2025, and Next_Step logs confirm Ariba is under active review for replacement ('on top of Ariba/to be reviewed', 28 January 2026). Ariba is the platform Zycus must displace to win the S2P opportunity, but displacing an incumbent is a MEDIUM threat because the buyer has already signaled replacement intent. (3) Do-nothing / status quo is LOW/DORMANT: Michelle stated 'we don't have any concrete timeline' and the current 'shield' tool is 'still under pilot phase', signaling inertia is real but not preferred. The wedge against Ariba is AI/Merlin capabilities and integrated S2P + ESG/risk (single system vs Ariba + separate risk tool); the wedge against the unnamed risk tool is the same integrated platform value, but this wedge is UNACTIVATED because the dual-track misalignment has not been resolved.
